messages.content:
The consequences of the direct impact of acid precipitation on the human body include...


a.
Alopecia.


b.
Skin and mucous membranes Irritation.


c.
Intake of heavy metal ions in the food chain.

d. Respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis.

e. Damage to the eyes, leading to irritation and potential vision problems.

f. Increased risk of heart and lung diseases.

g. Weakening of the bones and teeth due to the leaching of minerals from the body.

h. Potential development of neurological disorders from exposure to heavy metals.

i. Increased risk of cancer due to exposure to toxic substances in acid precipitation.

The consequences of the direct impact of acid precipitation on the human body include...choose one or more correct options:


a.
Alopecia.


b.
Skin and mucous membranes Irritation.


c.
Intake of heavy metal ions in the food chain.

b. Skin and mucous membranes Irritation.

c. Intake of heavy metal ions in the food chain.

What do you consider are true...

a.
The main destructors of the ozone layer are carbon oxides.

b.
Compounds of chlorine, nitrogen, hydrogen in the atmosphere cause the destruction of stratospheric ozone.

c.
One of the mechanisms of the cytoxic effect of carbon monoxide on the human body is the inhibition of catalase activity.

b. Compounds of chlorine, nitrogen, hydrogen in the atmosphere cause the destruction of stratospheric ozone.

c. One of the mechanisms of the cytoxic effect of carbon monoxide on the human body is the inhibition of catalase activity.

What do you consider are true...choose one correct option:

a.
The main destructors of the ozone layer are carbon oxides.

b.
Compounds of chlorine, nitrogen, hydrogen in the atmosphere cause the destruction of stratospheric ozone.

c.
One of the mechanisms of the cytoxic effect of carbon monoxide on the human body is the inhibition of catalase activity.
b. Compounds of chlorine, nitrogen, hydrogen in the atmosphere cause the destruction of stratospheric ozone.

Atmospheric air pollution with nitrogen oxides contributes to..


a.
Disruption of the photosynthesis process in plants.


b.
Ozone depletion


c.
The development of photochemical smog.
c. The development of photochemical smog.

Factors that enhance the decay reaction of ozone molecules in the ozone layer are...


a.
Hydroxide and hydrogen hydroperoxide


b.
Sulfur and nitrogen oxides


c.
Polychlorinated biphenyls
b. Sulfur and nitrogen oxides

Atmospheric air pollution with sulfur dioxide causes...


a.
Metabolic disorders in conifers


b.
The formation of acid precipitation.


c.
Ozone layer depletion.
b. The formation of acid precipitation.

Atmospheric gases are divided into such groups as...


a.
unstable to anthropogenic influences.


b.
changing.


c.
resistant to anthropogenic influences
c. resistant to anthropogenic influences

What effect have acid precipitation on the human body...

a.
Indirect.

b.
Cause alkalosis.

c.
Direct
c. Direct

Why the ozone layer is able to reduce the intensity of ultraviolet radiation (UV radiation).


a.
UVR energy is used for photochemical reactions in the stratosphere


b.
Some energy of UV radiation is used to form and destruct ozone in the stratosphere. 


c.
Ultraviolet photons are absorbed by tropospheric ozone molecules.
b. Some energy of UV radiation is used to form and destruct ozone in the stratosphere.

Tropospheric ozone on our planet ...

a.
Is Required for photosynthesis.

b.
Absorbs the hard ultraviolet sun radiation.

c.
Is an active methemoglobin former.
b. Absorbs the hard ultraviolet sun radiation.
